授業目的: This is an introductory coruse to Business Ethics for undergraduate students in management sciences. Pedagogically, it is suited to a multicultural classroom environment with domestic and international students and, thus, the language of instruction is English. On the one hand, the CSR module builds on a number of CSR definitions that share a concern for how enterprises respond to stakeholders. Thus, the CSR module aims to shed light on the forces that shape enterprises with separation of ownership and control, and around which stakeholders with different claims and duties interact. On the other hand, the Business ethics module adopts a normative approach to address the ethical challenges arising from the need to reconcile profitability with the pursuit of morally acceptable objectives. Both modules build upon a mix of timeless academic contributions and textbooks.
到達目標: Graduates will be capable of identifying the overall stakes of a situation, analysing different options, and assessing their ethical implications so as to take responsible decisions in leadership and management positions.Graduates will be able to understand the stakeholders capitalism model’s implicaitons in terms of ethical decision-making. They will make the bridge with CSR theories.Graduates will know the ethical principles and rules applied to different positions/functions within the firm (marketing, finance, HR ….).
関連科目: Basic management, Management A&B, Japanese corporate governance
授業の概要・背景: This course provides an introductory yet systematic overview of Business Ethics for undergraduate students in a multicultural classroom environment (domestic and international students). It examines why ethics has become central to business decision-making and organizational management, and how firms can reconcile profitability with morally acceptable objectives. The course combines foundational frameworks (e.g., ethics vs. compliance, normative ethics, ethical dilemmas, behavioral ethics) with applied domains (marketing, finance, AI, and environmental ethics). Learning is supported by a mix of short lectures, guided discussions, case analyses, and a “serious game” simulation to develop practical moral reasoning and responsible decision-making skills.
授業方法: The course is delivered in English and consists of:Face-to-face sessions (90 minutes each): short lectures (theoretical foundations) plus structured discussion based on assigned readings and case materials.Active learning components: small-group work, guided discussion, and a serious-game simulation (e.g., ethics committee role-play / mock trial-style exercise).Flex / on-demand learning: designated asynchronous sessions using short videos, readings, and case-based tasks to reinforce and extend in-class learning.Students are expected to prepare assigned readings in advance and actively participate in discussions and team-based activities.【Important】Face-to-face session dates (and class period)This course consists of 13 face-to-face sessions (details are provided below). All face-to-face sessions are scheduled in the 2nd period on the following dates:September 29 & 30 (Tue/Wed, 2nd period)October 6 & 7 (Tue/Wed, 2nd period)October 13 & 14 (Tue/Wed, 2nd period)October 20 & 21 (Tue/Wed, 2nd period)October 27 & 28 (Tue/Wed, 2nd period)November 4 (Wed, 2nd period)November 10 & 11 (Tue/Wed, 2nd period)Note: The Startup Week (online, on-demand) and the two flex-hour sessions (online, on-demand) are provided in addition to the face-to-face sessions listed above.All necessary files are shared via the following Box link:https://kwansei.box.com/s/v8knao333xfbu6cqzlp4yip9turwgdyt
